summary: See clues file for full details. 2 tossups, 8 bonuses found.

works.name: General / Key Works
works.description: Analysis from 2 tossups and 8 bonuses. See clues file for detailed breakdown.

clues.clue: One of these paintings prominently features a statue of Diana of Ephesus, which towers above a figure seated in the background.
examples: One of these paintings prominently features a statue of Diana of Ephesus, which towers above a figure seated in the background.

clues.clue: That painting also features a black panther in the foreground, while another of these paintings seats the black panther at the central figure's feet.
examples: That painting also features a black panther in the foreground, while another of these paintings seats the black panther at the central figure's feet.

clues.clue: Outlines in white and black were added to one of these paintings to depict a (*) tattooed version of its subject.
examples: Outlines in white and black were added to one of these paintings to depict a (*) tattooed version of its subject.

clues.clue: In all of these paintings, the central figure stretches her left arm, pointing towards the right side of the canvas.
examples: In all of these paintings, the central figure stretches her left arm, pointing towards the right side of the canvas.

clues.clue: Floating heads in some of these paintings were supposedly inspired by ukiyo-e prints copied by its artist.
examples: Floating heads in some of these paintings were supposedly inspired by ukiyo-e prints copied by its artist.

clues.clue: A polyptych by this artist contains a three-by-three grid of panels whose rows depict the Golden, Silver, and Iron Ages, with each age represented by Adam, Hesiod and Orpheus, and Cain, respectively.
examples: A polyptych by this artist contains a three-by-three grid of panels whose rows depict the Golden, Silver, and Iron Ages, with each age represented by Adam, Hesiod and Orpheus, and Cain, respectively.

clues.clue: Like most of his best-known works, his The Life of Humanity is located in his house-museum in Paris.
examples: Like most of his best-known works, his The Life of Humanity is located in his house-museum in Paris.

clues.clue: An unfinished work by this artist was inspired by Carpaccio's Ten Thousand Martyrs of Ararat.
examples: An unfinished work by this artist was inspired by Carpaccio's Ten Thousand Martyrs of Ararat.

clues.clue: This artist of The Chimeras painted a woman wearing a revealing dress made up almost entirely of jewels, who raises her arm toward a floating, haloed head with blood pouring from its neck.
examples: This artist of The Chimeras painted a woman wearing a revealing dress made up almost entirely of jewels, who raises her arm toward a floating, haloed head with blood pouring from its neck.

clues.clue: This friend of Theodore (*) Chasseriau placed a Greek god at the top of a massive throne garlanded with flowers in a work in which a woman with an open wound in her thigh recoils at the glory of her divine lover.
examples: This friend of Theodore (*) Chasseriau placed a Greek god at the top of a massive throne garlanded with flowers in a work in which a woman with an open wound in her thigh recoils at the glory of her divine lover.

clues.clue: This artist also painted a mythical beast climbing up a Theban man's body.
examples: This artist also painted a mythical beast climbing up a Theban man's body.
